media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot

characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The main protagonist and the "teenage robot" (built five years ago) to which the title refers, a state-of-the-art sapient gynoid automaton created by Dr. Noreen Wakeman five years prior to the series, though she was designed as a teenage girl. Though XJ-9 was designed to be Earth's protector, armed to the teeth with a wide range of weapons, devices, and transformations,she wants to live the life of a normal teenagerand often makes this desire quite apparent to her friends and creator. Action Girl: A ridiculously powerful crime-fighting robot. Age-Inappropriate Dress: Taking into account her body parts that count as "clothing", she appears to have a crop top, a miniskirt, and knee-high boots. Not an outfit that would normally be seen as formal. The Ageless: Since Jenny is a robot, she'll look like a teenager forever, though she does seem capable of maturing mentally. She even has a dream of hanging out with her classmates when they're in the retirement home and she looks no different than the present. Ain't Too Proud to Beg: Typically to her mother when she doesn't get her way.

characters.name: Jenny
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The main protagonist and the "teenage robot" (built five years ago) to which the title refers, a state-of-the-art sapient gynoid automaton created by Dr. Noreen Wakeman five years prior to the series, though she was designed as a teenage girl. Though XJ-9 was designed to be Earth's protector, armed to the teeth with a wide range of weapons, devices, and transformations,she wants to live the life of a normal teenagerand often makes this desire quite apparent to her friends and creator. Action Girl: A ridiculously powerful crime-fighting robot. Age-Inappropriate Dress: Taking into account her body parts that count as "clothing", she appears to have a crop top, a miniskirt, and knee-high boots. Not an outfit that would normally be seen as formal. The Ageless: Since Jenny is a robot, she'll look like a teenager forever, though she does seem capable of maturing mentally. She even has a dream of hanging out with her classmates when they're in the retirement home and she looks no different than the present. Ain't Too Proud to Beg: Typically to her mother when she doesn't get her way.

characters.name: Brad
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Jenny's best friend and next-door neighbor. Action Survivor: He's a completely normal human, but he helps Jenny take on intergalactic threats regularly, and even sometimes joins her on the front lines. Anime Hair: He has three large spikes in his hair. He uses a lot of hair gel to get it that way. Badass Normal: No super powers to speak of, but still occasionally manages to be useful in battle. Bumbling Sidekick: Most of the time when he tries to get in on the action and help his best friend fight crime, he usually screws up and XJ-9 has savehimand the day. But he can be quite capable at times.

characters.name: Tuck
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Brad's little brother. Absurd Phobia: In "Raggedy Android", he tries to face his fears on the Ferris Wheel. He's not afraid of heights or anything: just giant wheels. Annoying Younger Sibling: Tuck can sometimes be annoying, mostly because he's such ascreamking, though it doesn't seem to hurt his popularity in-universe. Brad even claims, when talking about Kilgore and Tuck, that "annoying + small = popular". Badass Adorable: He always going on adventures with his brother and knows almost as much as Dr. Wakeman about robots. Bratty Half-Pint: He's a real nuisance to Jenny, Sheldon, and Brad.

characters.name: Dr. Wakeman
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: An elderly spinster robotics scientist and the one who built the XJ robots, who is often simply refered to as "Mother" or "Mom" by Jenny and her sisters. Abusive Parents: To the other models of the XJ line, who shekeeps deactivated in the basement for being imperfect. They are notably gleeful to get the chance to attack her when reactivated and used by theVillain of the Week. Action Mom: When in danger, she shows no reluctance in fighting. Amazingly Embarrassing Parent: Any time Nora talks to a peer of Jenny besides Brad and Tuck, expect her to share some embarrassing detail about Jenny's design or maintenance. Badass Adorable: Is fairly short and cute, if a bit reserved, but she is a bonafideAction Momand Skyway Patrol veteran.

characters.name: Sheldon
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Jenny's self-proclaimed romantic admirer. Abhorrent Admirer: Towards Jenny at first,but he mellows out of his stalking in later episodes. Ace Pilot: He's shown to be capable of flying alien spaceships competently. Agent Peacock: When he joins the secret agency, his codename is "Baron Von Fashionista III". All of the Other Reindeer: Sheldon Lee is shown to be an outcast from the other students. In his first appearance in his episode, he was bullied in class in front of a teacher, though the teacher appeared to think that Sheldon‘s bullies were his friends and they were playing. When Sheldon gains a crush on Jenny for protecting him from bullies and follows her around, all the other students makes fun of both of them and a teacher sees it and only tells the other kids to wait until class is over to mock them, implying that Sheldon is extremely unpopular at school for being a nerd. Eventually, after being annoyed by Sheldon and telling him off and Sheldon still saving Jenny, she accepted him as a friend. Sheldon’s unpopularity persisted in the first few episodes of the season, in which he was unpopular with the popular kids and picked on by them, with only Jenny and Brad accepting him. But later in Seasons 2 and 3, Sheldon‘s popularity seems to rise a bit, as he is shown interacting with other kids with them not minding talking to him, and he forges a strong friendship with Jenny, Brad, and Tuck, even if Jenny is still annoyed by his obsessive crush on her. It appears that now only popular kids dislike him, but it is still better than his first appearance, where it was implied that he didn’t have any friends.

characters.name: XJ-Sisters
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: XJ-9's prototypes and "sisters". Baby Talk: XJ-1 only babbles, cries, and whimpers like an infant. Big Little Sister: XJ-8 is much larger than Jenny despite being considered her younger sister. She is technicallyalsoolder (which would make XJ-1 theLittle Big Sister), though Jenny seems to have spent the most time online. Brawn Hilda: XJ-8 is enormous, bulky and speaks in a deepMachine Monotone, overall causing her to appear very masculine. She retains theGirlish Pigtailsand miniskirt shared by many of her sisters, however, and is always referred to with female pronouns. Break Out the Museum Piece: Once, after Jenny got captured by the Cluster, she sent out an emergency beacon that reactivated them. With the exception of XJ-8 they don't appear to be combat-ready, but do okay as they apparently have the sametelescoping armorJenny does.

characters.name: Vexus
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The main antagonist, and the tyrannical queen of the Cluster who constantly tries to get Jenny to go with her to her homeworld of Cluster Prime through immoral means. Abusive Parents: She stole the powers of all the robots on Cluster Prime and that included Vega, her daughter. Animal Motif: She and the Cluster Army clearly resembles insects, Vexus in-particular resembles that of an Egyptian Sacred Scarab with herNepharious Pharaohappearance. Arch-Enemy: Vexus to Jenny, and vice-versa. Vexus isn't even happy with conquering Earth unless Jenny is there to see it. Attention Whore: She's devolved into this by the Season 3 episode "Queen Bee". After losing her planet, she's overjoyed at the idea of being adored by everyone at Tremorton High.

characters.name: Smytus
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A large green robot from the Cluster who seems to have ties to its military division. Badass Boast:Brad:We can do it! We can stop them! We haven't met a robot yet we can't destroy!Smytus:Then you haven't metme! Bad Boss: His answer to one incompetent minion causing him to lose the Pip crystals?Throw his entire crew out the airlock. Baritone of Strength: Comes with being voiced by Steve Blum. Big Creepy-Crawlies: In keeping with the Cluster's bug-theme, Smytus resembles a large rhinoceros beetle.

characters.name: Krackus
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A senile, older Cluster robot who often bungles his experiments and forgets where he is all the time. His inventions almost never last for very long, but he's not one to give up easily. Beware the Silly Ones: He may be an old coot with a failing memory, but he's still a capable inventor, even if some of his inventions don’t work. Like what Nora tells her daughter, "Don't underestimate him! Sooner or later, he may invent something that actually works!" Big Creepy-Crawlies: He bears a resemblance to a large beetle or cockroach. Bungling Inventor: He's pretty clumsy and most of his inventions fall apart after being used once. Evil Genius: He's the brains of the Cluster, though everyone else believes otherwise.

characters.name: Ambassador
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A huge Cluster robot who appears in "Doom With A View". He acts as a messenger to invite Jenny to join the empire, and is the first member of the Cluster she encounters. Achilles' Heel: He has a largeoff buttonon his back. Affably Evil: To Jenny, at least. He's much more genuinely courteous and professional toward Jenny than the other Cluster villains of the series, even as he fights with and tries to forcibly abduct her. Aggressive Negotiations: The Ambassador has evidently been told to never take "no" for an answer. When Jenny rejects his invitation to the Cluster, he attempts to take her by force. "Ass" in "Ambassador": His actual job is not to negotiate, but to assimilate robots across the universe into the Cluster by force.

characters.name: Killgore
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A small, adorable wind up robot from a toyline, Killgore somehow managed to attain sapience and decides to fulfill its purpose as a villain, first by joining the Cluster and capturing Jenny, then by destroying the ClusterandJenny. Things don't always go as planned for him, however. Aborted Arc: "Enclosure of Doom" ends with him getting away from Jenny and leaving a tape recorder for her with a message that he'll destroy her someday soon. However, he never appears in another episode. It’s possible hemighthave appeared again, if the show hadn't beenCut Short. Badass Adorable: He managed to outsmart Jenny twice andrebuild Armagedroid, who ismillionsof times Killgore's size. Bait-and-Switch Character Intro: His first scene introduces him as adeep-voicedsilhouetted seemingly-hulking figure only for the lights in the toy store he's in to be turned on to reveal his bright color scheme, and the shop owner walks over and winds him up to reveal he's not only tiny, but his real voice is very high-pitched. Berserk Button: He hates being called cute. Too bad he is.

characters.name: Armagedroid
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A gigantic robot created by Dr. Wakeman back when she was a Skyway Patrol member for the purpose of eliminating alien weaponry. It turned its attention to human weapons in peacetime, forcing Dr. Wakeman to lure it into the Earth's core where it remained- for the time being. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Its directive was to destroy weapons, and when it defeated an armada of hostile aliens, it turned its fangs on humanity, believing peace required destroyinganythingof theirs that might be considered a weapon. Lacking any capacity for independent/humanlike thought, Armagedroid technically was still doingexactly what he was created to do... only on amuch larger scalethan what Dr. Wakeman intended, as his ultimate goal was essentially to rid the Earth of weaponry. Back from the Dead: After getting blown up, Killgoresomehowrebuilt him entirely. Cain and Abel: The Cain to the entire XJ series, being an earlier Wakeman prototype that has a vaguely similar color scheme. Corrupted Character Copy: Armagedroid is a corrupt version ofThe Iron Giantfrom his movie. The two of them are giant robots that were designed to destroy weapons. The two of them, programming to destroy weapons, can make them dangerous to the people around them. But the difference is that, despite the Iron Giant's being a danger to the people around him because of his programming to destroy weapons, he was ultimately a good, childlike robot who wanted to live in peace with humans and eventually bonded with a human child. He eventually overcame his programming to stop himself from hurting people. Armageddon, in contrast, was too single-minded in its programming to destroy weapons. After beating an alien invasion, he became unstable in peacetime and started to turn against Earth, destroying his creator’s weapons, with it being made clear that he doesn’t care if lives are lost. Interestingly, the two of them have an opposite character arc. The Iron Giant was implied to be built for malicious intent and programmed to des

characters.name: The Space Bikers
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A biker gang of five fish-like aliens who come to Earth every so often to cause chaos. They are Letta, Sludge, Olga, Tammy, and Lenny. Abhorrent Admirer: Tammy is revealed to be attracted to Brad in "Ball and Chain" and even attempts to marry him. For the most part, Brad is very uncomfortable about Tammy's (violent) affections. He actually turned around for a while and saw it as an opportunity for adventure, until he realized she'd always treat him like a slave—and then eat him when they broke up. Aerith and Bob: Tammy, Olga, Letta and Lenny are real names, but Sludge is not. All Bikers Are Hells Angels: They're first introduced as a gang of thugs who spend their lives partying and wreaking havoc in orderly places, butin "Voyage to the Planet of the Bikers", it's revealed they're only like that on weekends and live as schoolteachers on their homeworld. Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: They are female aliens with stereotypical "bad girl" outfits. Two of them, Olga and Sludge, wear clothes that leave their midsections exposed.They're also the ugliest two out of the five.

characters.name: Himcules
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A super-powerful bully who gets stronger by inflicting physical and emotional pain in people around him. Berserk Button: Anything that calls his masculinity into question, most notably laughing at him or call his toga a dress. Brought to You by the Letter "S": He wears a gold medallion with a black "H" on his toga. The Bully: Taken to extreme levels, as bullying people literally makes him stronger.Thin-Skinned Bully: What he ultimately proves to be. When Jenny literally laughs off his attempts to bully her, and the whole town joins in mocking him, Himcules turns into a stereotypical nerd. Thin-Skinned Bully: What he ultimately proves to be. When Jenny literally laughs off his attempts to bully her, and the whole town joins in mocking him, Himcules turns into a stereotypical nerd.

characters.name: Dr. Locus
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: An evil mad scientist who is the father of Melody. The Bad Guy Wins: Nearly defeating the heroes aside, Dr. Locus successfully accomplishes his objective in "Bradventure" by copying Jenny's abilities and installing them into hisdaughterMelody. Bald of Evil: He has no hair on his head and is one of the biggest threats in the series. Beard of Evil: He's an evil scientist and has a scruffy white beard. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Initially presents himself as a kindly old man to Brad, but quickly drops the act once he realizes he can use him as bait to trap XJ-9.

characters.name: Gigawatt
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: Being comprised of electricity makes him extremely vulnerable to water, which shorts him out. Beware the Silly Ones: He's quirky, but Gigawatt's very nature is to drain power, which is lethal to an electronic like Jenny. His powers make him a walking EMP capable of inducing blackouts to entire cities with his mere presence. Curb-Stomp Battle: Because he's living kryptonite for electronics, he easily defeats Jenny in "Mind Over Matter" multiple times, to the point she enlists help from her mother and friends to defeat him. Evil Is Bigger: His size increases as he absorbs more energy. Evil Is Hammy: His voice and personality are reminiscent of Dracula.

characters.name: The Exo-Skin
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Allegorical Character: The suit representthe dark side of Jenny's goalsfor social assimilation:Loss of Identity,stifling conformity, andheinous apathy to the people she's climbed past on the social ladder. Clingy Costume: Clingyskin. Upon gaining sapience, the skin wouldn't allow Jenny to simply remove it, even forcing itself back on in her sleep. Death of Personality: If it had its way, Jenny would still be the same beneath the skin, but unrecognizable in behavior. Expy: It's a sapient suit that eventually grows a mind of its own and doesn't like being separated from its host. Now, are we talking about the skin or Marvel'sVenom? Female Misogynist: The suit is not exactly female in a biological sense, but it has a woman's voice, combines with Jenny to look like a young woman, and has averyregressive idea of femininity.

characters.name: Lil Acorn
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A robotic ventriloquist dummy created by Professor Wakeman in her youth for a talent show. After his attempt to become a solo act backfired, he was abandoned in the wilderness for years after his batteries ran out, until one day he was brought back to life and sought to get revenge on his creator. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: He was created by Nora to be a dummy she wouldn't need to fully puppet around during her ventriloquism gig. He would end up developing an inflated ego, leading to him abandoning her and attempting to go solo. When the public rejected him, he went on a rampage before running off into the wilderness. Upon returning, he attempts to get revenge on his creator after going even further insane from loneliness. Beware the Silly Ones: Despite having his weapons replaced, he still has his programming as a defense robot and is able to use his various comedy props to deadly effect in his fight against Jenny. Demonic Dummy: As Lil Acorn's ego grew he became meaner and meaner, abusing young Nora behind the scenes and refusing to be deactivated. It came back to bite him whe he tried to go solo, but got chased off by an angry mob who found a living dummy to be too creepy. I Just Want to Be Loved: More than anything else, he wants companionship and love. Even Jenny cannot bring herself to hate him because she realized he was just lonely.

characters.name: The Crust Cousins (Brit, Tiff)
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The antagonists of Jenny's life in high school, they seek to humiliate her at every turn. Alpha Bitch: They torment Jenny simply because a super-powered robotwould otherwise be more popular than them. Ambiguously Brown: It's not stated what Brit's ethnic background is, but she has dark skin and a British accent. Given that her cousin Tiff speaks in African-American vernacular English, she can either be an African-American anglophilic girl or that she's an Afro-British foreign exchange student. Alternately, she's still a British exchange student, but ofIndiandescent (which would not be unusual given the history of the British Raj). Tiff herself is an inverted case of this, as she is voiced by black actressCree Summerbut it’s unclear whether Tiff is meant to be a white girl or a light skinned black girl. Attention Whore: There was a whole episode that revolved around these two setting Jenny up to be splashed in oil because Jenny was getting more attention than them in her new design. Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: Tiff normally wears midriff-baring clothes to emphasize herAlpha Bitchnature, and she's the girlier of the duo. Brit is a lot more boyish in her choices of clothing, but some of her fashions in "Victim of Fashion" are midriff-baring as well.

characters.name: Don Prima
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The most popular boy in school. Jenny used to have a crush on him. Beauty Is Bad: A total hunk, but also a total douche. Big Jerk on Campus: He looks down on people while being the most popular. The Casanova: Implied to have gone on brief dates with most girls in the school. A Day in the Limelight: Is usually a minor background character, but he was prominent in the Season 1 episodes "The Great Unwashed" and especially "Saved By The Shell".

characters.name: Pteresa
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The best friend of Brit and Tiff who's often seen accompanying them. Berserk Button: When people pronounce the "p" in her name. Beta Bitch: Seems to be next in the social status after Brit and Tiff A Day in the Limelight: She's usually just in the background or aSatellite Characterto Brit and Tiff alongside others like her friends Jantrice and Stephanie, but in "The Price of Love" she plays a major role as Sheldon's fake date. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: She loves Killgore.

characters.name: Ichabod Razinski
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Adults Are Useless: Not only is he incapable of dealing with a fortress-scale threat, but he doesn't even try to sang the bullying at his school. Dean Bitterman: He's picked on Jenny multiple times for problems she's accidentally created. Eye Glasses: His thickNerd Glassesare unified with his eyeballs. Gag Nose: His nose is big red and hooked.

characters.name: Sebastian
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Future Loser: In 20 years he will be a felon and his wife Pterese will have to pay his bail permanently. Huge Guy, Tiny Girl: With Pteresa, he's at least twice her size. Jerk Jock: Consists on school soccer team and doesn't seem like a nice person. Satellite Love Interest: He doesn't have any notable roles other than dating Pteresa.

characters.name: Other Girls
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Asian Rudeness: Jentrice may be of Asian descent judging by her skin tone and eye cut and she's just as intolerant a douchebag as the rest of them. Girl Posse:They are all friends with their Crust cousins and Pteresa and agree with them on everything. Hime Cut: Jentrice has long, straight bangs that cover her forehead and eyebrows. Sexy Sweater Girl: Tracy wears a tight-fittingpurple sweater.

characters.name: Vega
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A citizen of Cluster Prime who seems wholly non-confrontational. Action Girl: She proves to be a capable fighter after unlocking her powers. Badass Adorable: She's an cute robot-girl who's capable of fighting after unlocking her powers. Blade Below the Shoulder: Once she installs her chip, she's able to access her weapons, including these. Cute Machines: She is as adorable as Jenny.

characters.name: Misty
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: An alien girl with superpowers who met Jenny as part of the Team Teen alongside Orion and Squish. The team later disbanded, and Misty returned to Earth. Alternate Company Equivalent: Team Teen is an homage toTeen Titans, and Mistry specifically resembles Raven in looks and powers, though her personality is almost the opposite. Badass Adorable: A perky goth girl who is one of the most powerful characters in the entire show. Bifauxnen: One of the more (tom)boyish-looking girls on the show. Curtains Match the Windows: Purple hair and purple eyes.

characters.name: Melody
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: The gentle daughter of aMad Scientistnamed Dr. Locus. She has a crush on Brad. Berserk Button: She does NOT like being called "weird". This makes her lose her friendliness and get into a massive fight with Jenny when she presses it. The Big Damn Kiss: Gives this to Brad to ease him up about her being a robot. It works. Creepy Blue Eyes: To add to the creepiness, herpupilsare blue while her iris is black. Effective Knockoff:She's essentially an improved version of Jenny. She's got a flawless exoskin, can self-repair and managed to outdo her in a fight.

characters.name: Dr. Mogg
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A scientist who is the rival of Nora Wakeman, and creator of YK-9 (AKA Kenny). Amazing Technicolor Population: He has a light green skin complexion. Beard of Evil: Downplayed; he's not evil, but Mogg can be bit of a jerk. Good Parents: While he might be a complete jerk, he's at least a well-meaning and caring father towards his creation and "son" Kenny. Green and Mean: He's has a greenish-skin tone and is a jerk, especially towards his rival Nora.

characters.name: Kenny
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Phineas Mogg's son who's a male knockoff of Jenny. They briefly dated, but she was no longer interested once she realized he was part-dog. Amicable Exes: Though they never broke up on screen, it's safe to say he's no longer in a relationship with Jenny when she learned about the fact that he's is part-dog. Despite this, the next time they meet they appear to be on good terms. Animal Jingoism: Given that he's half-dog, he immediately chases Sheldon once he dresses up as a cat. Dating What Daddy Hates: Continues to date Jenny even though both their parents disprove of it. Mundane Utility: He's able to use his robot body to cook pizzas. It gets him really popular with the other kids.

characters.name: Wisteria
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: Dr. Nora Wakeman's sister who is a flower child and focuses on the natural world and biological in contrast with her mechanical-based genius sister. Big Little Sister: Maybe. It's not known whether Wisteria is the older or younger one. However, she is definitely taller than Nora is and Wisteria retained her hair color while Nora's is white. On the other hand, in the family album, a teenage Wisteria is shown right after a picture of seeing Nora in second grade. Earthy Barefoot Character: She's very into nature and biology, and being both that and a hippie, doesn't wear shoes. Gag Nose: Downplayed, as while it's long, it's way shorter that her sister's. Granola Girl: One of the things that set her apart from her sister, Nora.

characters.name: Glenn
characters.media_title: My Life as a Teenage Robot
characters.description: A strange little swamp-monster like guy, he was created by Wisteria Wakeman and she treats him like her son. As such, this makes him Nora's nephew and Jenny's cousin. Acrofatic: He's plump, but can put up a fight if he chooses to. Black Bead Eyes: Like with anyone who doesn't havepie-eyes, he has these instead. Combat Tentacles: He uses vines as these when he fought against Jenny in his sole appearance. Green Thumb: During his (fake) fight with Jenny, he is able to shapeshift his various limbs and parts of himself into different plants like vines and cacti.
